text:
Stop and Jot

On your own, stop and jot some ideas about what cause-effect statement we can make about the speaker and the experiments you have done?

There are a lot of ways that scientists ask questions about cause-and-effect relationships in systems. We could ask a question about the effect. We could ask a question about the change we need to make to get the effect. We could ask a how or why question about a cause-and-effect relationship we are pretty sure about.

  1. What is the effect we observe on the system (or a part of the system) when we make this change?
  2. What is the change we would need to make to get this effect? What part(s) is involved in this change?
  3. Does this change cause this effect? Or could both things be happening for another reason?
  4. How or why does this change cause this effect? (model or explanation)
Write 1-2 additional questions for the DQB about a cause-and-effect relationship in the speaker system. Be prepared to post your questions to our Driving Question Board.
